steps.title: Recognize the experimental logic
steps.content: If CO2 were truly an additional cue to midges, the trap with calls + CO2 would attract significantly more midges than the calls-only trap. The conclusion that CO2 is not a cue requires evidence that adding CO2 makes no difference—or actually reduces attraction.

steps.title: Find the result that proves CO2 is irrelevant
steps.content:
More midges were found in the trap that only played calls than in the trap that played calls and released carbon dioxide. shows more midges in the calls-only trap than in the calls + CO2 trap.

This proves that CO2 does not enhance midge attraction and therefore is not serving as an additional cue, directly supporting the conclusion.

The other three options all suggest CO2 either increases attraction or serves as a signal the midges actively respond to.

That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
